growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.agit: static (C. matruchotii and S. mitis in incubator); E. coli shaken at 200 rpm
growth_records.medium: defined medium, L-lactate as sole carbon source
growth_records.cite: Almeida et al. Bacterial multispecies interaction mechanisms dictate biogeographic arrangement between the oral commensals Corynebacterium matruchotii and Streptococcus mitis. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00115-23. [PMC10654079]
growth_records.snippet: C. matruchotii WT doubling time 12.83 h ± 1.3 h (Table 1); diminished growth rate...aerobically with L-lactate as the sole carbon source
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (12.83 h) reported directly in Table 1. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu=ln2/doubling_time=0.6931/12.83=0.0540 h-1. Exact defined-medium composition not given in main text (only 'L-lactate as the sole carbon source', aerobic, presumed 37 degC as used for routine culture of this strain elsewhere in paper). No quantitative lactate-uptake concentration/time data provided, so qS not calculable.

growth_records.agit: static (C. matruchotii and S. mitis in incubator); E. coli shaken at 200 rpm
growth_records.medium: defined medium, L-lactate as sole carbon source
growth_records.cite: Almeida et al. Bacterial multispecies interaction mechanisms dictate biogeographic arrangement between the oral commensals Corynebacterium matruchotii and Streptococcus mitis. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00115-23. [PMC10654079]
growth_records.snippet: C. matruchotii DeltalutABC doubling time 58.78 h ± 19.0 h (Table 1), t-test vs WT P=1.3e-5; significantly impaired in L-lactate utilization
growth_records.notes: lutABC operon deletion mutant, essential for L-lactate catabolism. Doubling time (58.78 h) reported directly in Table 1. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu=ln2/58.78=0.0118 h-1. Same aerobic L-lactate-sole-carbon-source condition as WT record; medium composition not detailed in main text.

growth_records.agit: static (C. matruchotii and S. mitis in incubator); E. coli shaken at 200 rpm
growth_records.medium: defined medium, L-lactate as sole carbon source
growth_records.cite: Almeida et al. Bacterial multispecies interaction mechanisms dictate biogeographic arrangement between the oral commensals Corynebacterium matruchotii and Streptococcus mitis. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00115-23. [PMC10654079]
growth_records.snippet: C. matruchotii DeltalutABC + empty vector (pCGL0243) doubling time 50.07 h ± 3.5 h (Table 1), t-test vs WT P=4.3e-6
growth_records.notes: Empty-vector control for the lutABC complementation experiment. Doubling time (50.07 h) reported directly in Table 1. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu=ln2/50.07=0.0138 h-1.

growth_records.agit: static (C. matruchotii and S. mitis in incubator); E. coli shaken at 200 rpm
growth_records.medium: defined medium, L-lactate as sole carbon source
growth_records.cite: Almeida et al. Bacterial multispecies interaction mechanisms dictate biogeographic arrangement between the oral commensals Corynebacterium matruchotii and Streptococcus mitis. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00115-23. [PMC10654079]
growth_records.snippet: C. matruchotii DeltalutABC + lutABC complement vector doubling time 13.45 h ± 9.8 h (Table 1), t-test vs WT P=1.6e-1 (not significant, i.e. restored to near-WT)
growth_records.notes: In-trans complementation of lutABC restores growth close to WT. Doubling time (13.45 h) reported directly in Table 1. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu=ln2/13.45=0.0515 h-1.

growth_records.agit: static (C. matruchotii and S. mitis in incubator); E. coli shaken at 200 rpm
growth_records.medium: defined medium, L-lactate as sole carbon source
growth_records.cite: Almeida et al. Bacterial multispecies interaction mechanisms dictate biogeographic arrangement between the oral commensals Corynebacterium matruchotii and Streptococcus mitis. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00115-23. [PMC10654079]
growth_records.snippet: A lutA deletion strain was also created and showed similar results (Table 1): C. matruchotii DeltalutA doubling time 32.06 h ± 1.8 h
growth_records.notes: Single-gene (lutA) deletion within the lutABC operon; intermediate phenotype vs full operon deletion. Doubling time (32.06 h) reported directly in Table 1. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu=ln2/32.06=0.0216 h-1.

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 3102 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/3102
